content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Ms. Jan M. Brunken 
Treasurer, O'Melveny & Myers Political 
Action Committee 
400 South Hope Street 
Los Angeles, CA 
90071-2899 
October 3, 1986 
RE: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. I-86-254 
Dear Ms. Brunken: 
Thank you for your letter of August 6, 1986, requesting 
advice concerning political action committee contributions to 
candidates and measures being voted upon in other states. 
QUESTION 
May a political action committee registered in California 
make contributions in support of and in opposition to candidates 
and measures being voted upon in other states? 
CONCLUSION 
A political action committee registered in California may 
make contributions to candidates and measures being voted upon 
in other states. 
FACTS 
In addition to supporting various California candidates and 
measures, the O'Melveny & Myers Political Action Committee (a 
registered California committee) also makes contributions in 
support of and in opposition to candidates and measures being 
voted upon in other states. 
You have been advised by various 
members of the Commission's staff that this activity is a 
permissible use of campaign funds under California law and that 
the expenditures must be reported on the committee's campaign 
disclosure statements. 
You have asked that the Commission 
confirm this past advice in writing. 
428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804~0807 • 
(916)322~5660 


Ms. Brunken 
Page Two 
ANALYSIS 
Chapter 5 of Division 9 of the Elections Code (Section 12400 
et seq.) limits the permissible uses of campaign funds. 
Use in 
out-of-state campaigns in not prohibited. 
If you have any 
additional questions concerning the permissible uses of campaign 
funds, I suggest you contact the State Attorney General's Public 
Inquiry unit at its toll-free number: 
800/952-5225. 
By 
DMG:MAK:cah 
Sincerely, 
Diane M. Griffiths 
General Counsel

OUR FILE N0MSER 
637,385-411 
Robert Leidigh, Esq. 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street 
Sacramento, CA 95814 
DGar I1.r. Leidigh: 
I am the Treasurer of the O'Melveny & Myers 
Political Action Committee (No. 802120) (the "OM&M PAC"), 
a recipient committee under California law. 
The OM&M PAC 
receives contributions from O'Melveny & Myers, a law firm, 
and makes expenditures supporting various California candi-
dates and measures. 
I have been told by various persons 
at the Fair Political Practices Commission that the OM&M 
PAC may expend its contributions in support of or opposition 
to candidates and measures on the election ballot in states 
other than California or candidates on the election ballot 
for a national office. 
I recognize that such expenditures 
would continue to be subject to the disclosure provisions 
of California law, as well as federal or applicable state 
law. 
I was told that I could request an opinion from you 
to that effect. 
Please direct your opinion on this matter to 
my attention at the above-named address. 
If you have any 
questions, feel free to give me a call. 
JMB: jh 
Sincerely,

Jan M. Brunken

of the O'Melveny & Myers 
Political Action Committee
